conversation.content: I'm a workplace newcomer. I have to give my first work report to my leader next week. I'm so nervous I can't sleep. What should I do?
Don't panic first. The core of a report is to explain the work clearly, not a perfect performance. Get the content framework right first.
How do I build the framework? I'm afraid I'll speak in a mess and the leader won't understand.
Use the 'Total-Split-Total' structure: First state core results, then list work content, problems encountered and solutions, and finally talk about future plans.
I'm afraid of running over time. The report is only 10 minutes.
Write a verbatim script in advance, time yourself practicing against it, delete unimportant content, and highlight the results.
What if the leader asks questions in the middle? I'm afraid I won't be able to answer.
Anticipate possible questions from the leader in advance and prepare answers. If you really can't answer, honestly say you will verify and give feedback later. Don't make things up.
Do I have to look at the leader the whole time during the report? Eye contact makes me nervous.
You don't need eye contact the whole time. Look at the position of the leader's glabella (between eyebrows). It shows engagement without the pressure of direct eye contact.

How long does it take for the registration to become effective? If I register, can I still use my insurance back home?

It usually becomes effective 1-2 working days after submission. In most regions, after registering, your health insurance can be used normally both at your current location and back home, so no issues.
If I need to go to another province temporarily for medical treatment, like going to a big city hospital, do I also need to register?

Yes, you also need to register. Choose the 'Temporary Off-site Medical Treatment' registration, which is generally valid for 3-6 months. Once registered, you can get direct settlement. Otherwise, the reimbursement rate will be significantly reduced.
